name: Nagastra
description: The Nagastra is a powerful divine weapon in Hindu mythology, often depicted as an arrow or projectile imbued with the essence and venom of serpents (Naga). Its origins vary across different texts, but it is generally believed to be a creation of divine craftsmanship, either forged by celestial artisans or bestowed upon worthy warriors by deities. The Nagastra is capable of inflicting devastating damage, often causing instant death or paralysis through its potent venom. It manifests as a multitude of serpents erupting from the projectile upon impact, constricting and poisoning the target. Its significance lies in its ability to overcome formidable foes and turn the tide of battles. The weapon's use is often associated with great responsibility and righteous intent, as its power can be easily misused. Skilled warriors like Arjuna and Karna were known to possess and wield the Nagastra, demonstrating its importance in epic narratives like the Mahabharata. The Nagastra is a symbol of both destructive force and divine intervention, representing the power of nature and the consequences of wielding such power.
subtitle: Serpent Arrow of Devastating Power
origin: Forged by divine artisans or bestowed by deities.
currentLocation: Mythological realms; symbolism present in temples

keyMyths.title: Karna's Nagastra
keyMyths.description: Karna possessed the Nagastra gifted to him by Indra, but he could only use it once. He aimed it at Arjuna but Krishna saved Arjuna's life, resulting in the death of Ghatotkacha instead.

keyMyths.title: Indra's Gift
keyMyths.description: Some accounts state that Indra, the king of gods, created or gifted the Nagastra to a worthy warrior. This highlights its divine origin and the favour bestowed upon its wielder.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: Epic narrative containing instances of the Nagastra's use.
sources.text: Epic narrative containing instances of the Nagastra's use.

sources.author: Various Authors
